findings.problem: The repaired docs still leave the roadblock-related reset/debug contract internally inconsistent: the post-reset baseline defines a diagnostic "roadblock source=`not_implemented`", while the retained/removed/added field contract exposes `roadblock_interface_state` with the reset-ready value family `supported_not_implemented`. The docs do not say whether these are the same field, two different public fields, or which value the post-reset snapshot must actually publish.
findings.evidence: /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/refactor-old-2-steering-control/design.md:207-211 defines post-reset diagnostic source fields including `roadblock source=not_implemented`. /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/refactor-old-2-steering-control/design.md:393-399 defines the `ControlDebugSnapshot::steering` / `control.steering_snapshot` public field contract, which includes `roadblock_interface_state` with `supported_not_implemented` and does not define a `roadblock source` field. /home/madejuele/projects/2K0300/openspec/changes/refactor-old-2-steering-control/tasks.md:49-51 requires a one-to-one retained/removed/added field contract plus a concrete post-reset clean baseline.
findings.recommendation: Reconcile the reset baseline and field table so the roadblock diagnostic contract uses one explicit public field name and one explicit reset value set. Either document a separate `roadblock_source` field in the retained/removed/added contract, or rewrite the baseline to use the existing `roadblock_interface_state` contract and its documented reset value consistently.
